steps.title: Identify the key cause-effect structure
steps.content:
The passage contains two key cause-effect claims: “The ice melted on a Norwegian mountain during a particularly warm summer in 2019, revealing a 1,700-year-old sandal to a mountaineer looking for artifacts.”

It also says, “The sandal would normally have degraded quickly, but it was instead well preserved for centuries by the surrounding ice.” This directly links temperature to both preservation (ice keeping it intact) and revelation (melting exposing it now).

steps.title: Match the dual role of temperature in the text
steps.content:
The correct answer directly addresses how temperature-related ice contributed to both protecting (preventing degradation) and revealing (melting) the sandal.

The other three choices either misinterpret the Roman comparison, ignore the public's role in discovery, or reverse cause and effect.

That confirms A is correct.
